react.answer:
## Building a Dual Range (Price) Slider in React

**Mental model:** Two thumbs (low/high) over one track. The trick is clamping: the low thumb can't pass the high and vice-versa. Two stacked `<input type="range">` give you native thumbs; a derived fill `<div>` between them (positioned by percentage) shows the selected band.

### Step 1 — Two values, clamped on change
```jsx
const [lo, setLo] = useState(20);
const [hi, setHi] = useState(80);
const onLo = (v) => setLo(Math.min(v, hi));
const onHi = (v) => setHi(Math.max(v, lo));
```

### Step 2 — Position the fill by percentage
```jsx
const pct = (v) => ((v - min) / (max - min)) * 100;
<div style={{ left: `${pct(lo)}%`, right: `${100 - pct(hi)}%` }} />
```

### Step 3 — Stack the two inputs
Both range inputs overlap the track; `pointer-events: none` on the track and `auto` on the thumbs lets each thumb stay grabbable.

```jsx
<input type="range" value={lo} onChange={(e) => onLo(+e.target.value)} />
<input type="range" value={hi} onChange={(e) => onHi(+e.target.value)} />
```

### Step 4 — Show the readout
```jsx
<output>${lo} – ${hi}</output>
```

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Thumbs cross | Clamp each against the other on change. |
| Equal values | Allowed (zero-width band) — clamp uses `<=`. |
| Overlapping hit areas | Raise z-index of the thumb nearer the cursor for the crossed case. |

**Interview tip:** The cross-over clamp (`Math.min(v, hi)` / `Math.max(v, lo)`) is the whole problem — say it explicitly, and mention the `pointer-events` layering trick that keeps both native thumbs usable when stacked.

vue.answer:
## Building a Dual Range (Price) Slider in Vue 3

**Mental model:** Two thumbs (low/high) over one track. The core is clamping so the thumbs can't cross. Two stacked `<input type="range">` give native thumbs; a fill `<div>` positioned by percentage shows the band.

### Step 1 — Two values, clamped
```vue
<script setup>
import { ref } from 'vue';
const lo = ref(20);
const hi = ref(80);
const onLo = (v) => { lo.value = Math.min(v, hi.value); };
const onHi = (v) => { hi.value = Math.max(v, lo.value); };
</script>
```

### Step 2 — Fill by percentage
```js
const pct = (v) => ((v - props.min) / (props.max - props.min)) * 100;
```

### Step 3 — Stack the inputs
`pointer-events: none` on the inputs, `auto` on the thumbs (via `::-webkit-slider-thumb`) keeps both grabbable.

```vue
<input type="range" :value="lo" @input="onLo(+$event.target.value)" />
<input type="range" :value="hi" @input="onHi(+$event.target.value)" />
```

### Step 4 — Readout
```vue
<output>${{ lo }} – ${{ hi }}</output>
```

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Thumbs cross | `Math.min`/`Math.max` clamp each. |
| Equal values | Allowed (zero-width band). |
| Hit areas | Layer thumb z-index for the crossed case. |

**Interview tip:** The clamp pair is the entire challenge; mention the `pointer-events` layering needed because two stacked range inputs would otherwise steal each other's drags.

angular.answer:
## Building a Dual Range (Price) Slider in Angular

**Mental model:** Two thumbs (low/high) over one track; clamp so they can't cross. Two stacked `<input type="range">` give native thumbs; a fill `<div>` positioned by percentage shows the band. Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them), no `FormsModule`.

### Step 1 — Two values, clamped
```typescript
lo = 20;
hi = 80;
onLo(v: number): void { this.lo = Math.min(v, this.hi); }
onHi(v: number): void { this.hi = Math.max(v, this.lo); }
```

### Step 2 — Fill by percentage
```typescript
pct(v: number): number { return ((v - this.min) / (this.max - this.min)) * 100; }
```

### Step 3 — Stack the inputs
Bind `[value]` + `(input)`; CSS gives `pointer-events: none` to the input and `auto` to the thumb pseudo-element.

```html
<input type="range" [value]="lo" (input)="onLo(toNum($any($event.target).value))" />
<input type="range" [value]="hi" (input)="onHi(toNum($any($event.target).value))" />
```

### Step 4 — Readout
```html
<output>${{ lo }} – ${{ hi }}</output>
```

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Thumbs cross | `Math.min`/`Math.max` clamp. |
| Equal values | Allowed (zero-width band). |
| Hit areas | Layer thumb z-index for the crossed case. |

**Interview tip:** The clamp pair is the crux; the `pointer-events` layering (`none` on the input, `auto` on `::-webkit-slider-thumb`) is what lets two stacked native range inputs both stay draggable.
